kill_fact: The catalyst is genuinely live, dated and board-approved (Jan 4, 2027 spin of Mission Technology Solutions), independently confirmed against the KBR IR press release dated June 25, 2026 and the Sep 24, 2025 8-K on SEC EDGAR, so the event survives and the spread has NOT closed (stock ~$32-33, at/near 52-wk low $29.94). BUT two load-bearing pieces of the bull framing fail. (1) The 'real, defined-risk floor at ~$33' is overstated: this is a SPIN, not a merger-arb. There is no acquirer, no deal price, no contractual backstop. The '$33 floor' is merely the current trough trading multiple of a profitable bundle, a soft market-driven level that HAS ALREADY PRINTED BELOW ITSELF ($29.94 52-wk low) on sentiment alone - so the 'essentially no downside' claim is wrong; real downside is -10 to -15%. (2) This is a 2027 event whose gating regulatory step has barely begun: the Form 10 was still CONFIDENTIAL as of the Q1 FY2026 call (05/05/2026), with a public filing only anticipated ~September 2026 - so SEC-effectiveness is months away and the timeline already slipped one quarter from the original 'mid-to-late 2026' guidance. Underneath, MTS (the SpinCo half) is showing live softness: Q1 FY2026 revenue $1.923B (down ~5%), which threatens the clean 11-13x SpinCo re-rate the SoP assumes. The asymmetry is positive but rests on a sentiment-driven multiple re-rate, not a binding event payoff, and the floor is soft - this does not deserve a top conviction.
corrections: 1) Downside floor is NOT a defined-risk backstop (~$33.2); it is the trough trading price of a profitable bundle and has already printed $29.94 - model real downside to ~$28-30 (-10 to -15%), not ~flat. 2) Form 10 was CONFIDENTIAL as of 05/05/2026; public filing only anticipated ~Sep 2026, so the SEC-effectiveness forcing function is months away, not imminent. 3) Live MTS softness is real: Q1 FY2026 revenue $1.923B (down ~5%), supporting a possible weaker SpinCo launch multiple than the assumed 11-13x. 4) Treat the 'high-$70s full re-rate' as remote optionality, not a base case; base case correctly sits near Street ~$46-47 / dossier ~$53. SoP magnitude is plausible and the going-concern operating value is intact; only the floor's hardness and the catalyst's imminence are corrected.
evidence: KBR IR press release, June 25, 2026: 'The spin-off is expected to be completed on January 4, 2027... remains subject to customary conditions, including regulatory approvals and final approval by KBR's Board of Directors.' (kbr.com/en/insights-news/press-release/kbr-names-chief-executive-officer-and-chief-financial-officer-its-mission-technology-solutions-spin-business)

KBR Q1 FY2026 earnings call, 05/05/2026 - CEO Stuart Bradie: 'we are working toward an effective spin date of January 4, 2027'; on Form 10: 'We have confidentially resubmitted our Form 10 ... before transitioning to a public filing, which we currently anticipate in September' (fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/05/kbr-kbr-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/)

KBR 8-K, Exhibit 99.1 dated 09/24/2025 (SEC EDGAR CIK 0001357615): board unanimously approved strategic intent to spin off Mission Technology Solutions (~$5.8B revenue), tax-free, subject to Form 10 effectiveness and IRS opinion (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001357615/000135761525000053/exhibit99-109242025.htm)

Market data 06/19-06/22/2026 (stockanalysis.com/stocks/kbr/): price ~$32.6-32.8, 52-week low $29.94 (below the cited ~$33 'floor'), avg analyst target $46.57 (Buy) - spread still open, stock at trough multiple, floor not hard
